samples.sample_name: Massachusetts Department of Elementary and Secondary Education Special Education

query_basis: Reviewed 2026-06-25 the live official DESE district export and the live official Census TIGERweb county subdivision layer, then preserved a bounded county coverage audit from the exact export `Town` field.

samples.evidence_snippet: The official search form links to the same district query family that now also exposes a real export endpoint on the live DESE host.

The official `search_export.aspx` endpoint returns `application/excel` with a `search.xls` attachment that preserves `Org Name`, `Org Type`, `Function`, `Contact Name`, `Address 1`, `Town`, `State`, `Zip`, `Phone`, and `Grade` fields.

A bounded exact-basename join from the export `Town` field to the official county subdivision layer matched 406 rows directly and still covered all 14 Massachusetts counties, including Barnstable, Berkshire, Bristol, Dukes, Essex, Franklin, Hampden, Hampshire, Middlesex, Nantucket, Norfolk, Plymouth, Suffolk, and Worcester.

The live official TIGERweb county subdivision layer returns Massachusetts municipality basenames plus county codes, which supplied the reviewed town-to-county crosswalk used for the bounded county coverage audit.

query_basis: Verified ABLE program pages from the program spine.
Reviewed browser-readable DDS surfaces, the saved bounded locality-capture audit, a bounded official sitemap review, and a fresh exact raw 403 recheck on the live locations index.
blocker_evidence: Reviewed 2026-06-25 the live Massachusetts DDS first-party locations lane plus the saved bounded locality-capture audit, then rechecked the exact `/locations` endpoint again from the low-token raw lane. The public locations pages preserve 21 distinct `DDS ... Area Office` cards with explicit `This area office serves the following towns and communities:` text, and the reviewed town-to-county bridge already clears 13 of 14 counties from official browser-readable locality evidence. A bounded official sitemap review also surfaced exact first-party DDS area-office documents for Greater Boston/Metro Boston and Charles River West, but those files only preserve office identity, address, and phone and still do not publish counties served or town/community lists. The remaining gap is Suffolk County: bounded Boston, Chelsea, Revere, Winthrop, and Charlestown scans on the same official lane still do not preserve a Suffolk-serving town/community contract, while a fresh 2026-06-25 raw probe to `https://www.mass.gov/orgs/department-of-developmental-services/locations` still returns the same HTTP 403 `Not allowed` shell and therefore cannot supply a replayable county export. Massachusetts should stay blocked until an official Suffolk-serving DDS locality contract, county field, or county-grade export is preserved.

samples.evidence_snippet: The live DDS org page renders normally and links exact child surfaces including Contact a DDS Area Office, Find Your Regional and Area Office, and the department locations index.

The live locations index preserves 21 distinct `DDS ... Area Office` cards with explicit `This area office serves the following towns and communities:` text, and the reviewed town-to-county bridge already clears 13 of 14 Massachusetts counties from those official locality lists.

The live interactive map remains a real first-party town-or-city lookup lane, but bounded Suffolk scans across Boston, Chelsea, Revere, Winthrop, and Charlestown still do not preserve a Suffolk-serving locality contract that can clear the remaining county.

samples.evidence_snippet: The official Mass.gov sitemap exposes a first-party Greater Boston Area Office document that preserves `Metro Boston 65 Sprague Street, Rear West Hyde Park, MA 02136` and `(617) 363-2900`, which confirms the office identity and local address but still does not publish counties served or a Suffolk-only town/community contract.
The guessed `dds-area-offices` path is not the right live lane; it returns a real 404 `We can't find that page` response on Mass.gov.
